core.label: Kotlin Fundamentals
core.description: Syntax, types, null safety, and basic program structure.

topics.label: Setup & Tooling
topics.description: Install Kotlin with IntelliJ or the command-line compiler. Kotlin runs on the JVM, JS, or Native.

topics.label: Variables (val & var)
topics.description: Immutable val vs mutable var. Kotlin encourages immutability by default.

topics.label: Basic Types
topics.description: Int, Long, Double, Float, Boolean, Char, String. Everything is an object in Kotlin — no primitives in source code.

topics.label: String Templates
topics.description: Embed expressions in strings with $variable and ${expression}. Cleaner than string concatenation.

topics.label: Control Flow
topics.description: if (as expression), when (powerful switch), for, while. when replaces switch and is exhaustive for enums/sealed.

topics.label: Null Safety
topics.description: Nullable types (String?), safe calls (?.), Elvis operator (?:), and non-null assertion (!!). Kotlin eliminates NPEs.

topics.label: Functions
topics.description: Single-expression functions, default parameters, named arguments. Kotlin functions are concise and flexible.

topics.label: Collections
topics.description: List, Set, Map with mutable and immutable variants. Rich standard library for collection operations.

topics.label: Ranges & Progressions
topics.description: Create ranges with .., downTo, step. Ranges are used in for loops and contains checks.

topics.label: Type Checks & Smart Casts
topics.description: Check types with is and automatic smart casting. The compiler tracks type checks for safe access.

core.label: OOP & Classes
core.description: Classes, inheritance, data classes, sealed classes, and objects.

topics.label: Classes & Constructors
topics.description: Primary and secondary constructors, init blocks. Primary constructors are in the class header.

topics.label: Properties
topics.description: Val/var properties with custom getters/setters, backing fields, and lateinit.

topics.label: Inheritance & open
topics.description: Classes are final by default — use open for inheritance. Override methods with override.

topics.label: Interfaces
topics.description: Interfaces with abstract and default method implementations. A class can implement multiple interfaces.

topics.label: Data Classes
topics.description: Auto-generate equals, hashCode, toString, copy with data class. Perfect for DTOs and value objects.

topics.label: Sealed Classes & Interfaces
topics.description: Restrict class hierarchies for exhaustive when expressions. Ideal for state machines and result types.

topics.label: Object Declarations & Companions
topics.description: Singletons with object and static-like members with companion object. Kotlin has no static keyword.

topics.label: Enums
topics.description: Enum classes with properties, methods, and abstract members. Type-safe constants.

topics.label: Delegation
topics.description: Delegate interface implementation with by. Composition over inheritance made easy.

core.label: Functional Kotlin
core.description: Lambdas, higher-order functions, and collection operations.

topics.label: Lambda Expressions
topics.description: Concise anonymous functions with { params -> body }. Lambdas are used everywhere in Kotlin.

topics.label: Higher-Order Functions
topics.description: Functions that take or return functions. Foundation of Kotlin's functional style.

topics.label: Collection Operations
topics.description: map, filter, flatMap, groupBy, associate, zip, fold, reduce. Kotlin's collection API is exceptionally rich.

topics.label: Sequences
topics.description: Lazy evaluation with sequences for large collections. Sequences avoid intermediate collection allocation.

topics.label: Scope Functions
topics.description: let, run, with, apply, also. Scope functions execute code blocks on objects concisely.

topics.label: Extension Functions
topics.description: Add methods to existing classes without inheritance. Extension functions are Kotlin's killer feature.

topics.label: Destructuring
topics.description: Destructure data classes, pairs, and maps in declarations and lambdas.

topics.label: Inline Functions
topics.description: Eliminate lambda overhead with inline. Inline functions are important for performance-critical APIs.

core.label: Generics & Advanced Features
core.description: Generics, variance, coroutines introduction, and DSLs.

topics.label: Generics
topics.description: Type parameters, generic functions, and generic classes. Kotlin generics are similar to Java but with reified types.

topics.label: Variance (in/out)
topics.description: Declaration-site variance with in (contravariance) and out (covariance). Cleaner than Java's wildcards.

topics.label: Reified Type Parameters
topics.description: Access generic types at runtime with inline reified. Avoids type erasure limitations.

topics.label: Coroutines Basics
topics.description: Lightweight concurrency with suspend functions and coroutine builders (launch, async). Kotlin's async solution.

topics.label: Coroutine Scopes & Dispatchers
topics.description: CoroutineScope, GlobalScope, Dispatchers.IO/Default/Main. Scopes manage coroutine lifecycles.

topics.label: Flow
topics.description: Asynchronous cold streams with Flow. Kotlin's reactive streams built on coroutines.

topics.label: Type-Safe Builders (DSLs)
topics.description: Build domain-specific languages with lambdas with receivers. Used in Gradle, Ktor, and Compose.

topics.label: Operator Overloading
topics.description: Define custom operators with operator fun. Makes domain types expressive.

topics.label: Exception Handling
topics.description: try/catch/finally as expressions. Kotlin has no checked exceptions — all are unchecked.

topics.label: Result Type
topics.description: Wrap success/failure with Result<T>. Functional error handling without exceptions.

topics.label: Gradle with Kotlin DSL
topics.description: Build Kotlin projects with build.gradle.kts. Kotlin DSL provides type-safe build configuration.

topics.label: JUnit 5 & Kotlin
topics.description: Write tests with JUnit 5 and Kotlin's concise syntax. Test Kotlin idioms naturally.

topics.label: Kotest
topics.description: Kotlin-native testing framework with expressive matchers and property testing.

topics.label: MockK
topics.description: Kotlin-first mocking library. Supports coroutines, extension functions, and Kotlin-specific features.

topics.label: Java Interop
topics.description: Call Java from Kotlin and vice versa. Kotlin is 100% interoperable with Java.

topics.label: Kotlin Multiplatform
topics.description: Share code across JVM, JS, iOS, and Native. KMP enables true cross-platform development.
